# AI Coding Assistants Market Map

Use this note to explain the tool categories without turning the talk into a vendor comparison. The useful categories are IDE chat, terminal or repo agents, PR and CI review assistants, autonomous issue-to-PR agents, and enterprise control layers.

## Source Slice

- Source path: `research/hmrc-beyond-hype/import/AI coding assistants on 9 May 2026 for the HMRC Data Science Academy talk.md`.
- Source section: [State of the market](../../import/AI%20coding%20assistants%20on%209%20May%202026%20for%20the%20HMRC%20Data%20Science%20Academy%20talk.md#state-of-the-market).
- Source line range in the current import file: `18-99`.
- Source status: point-in-time public-source snapshot dated 9 May 2026; recheck fast-moving product claims before live use.

## Talk Use

- Explain the shift from suggest-code to complete-a-bounded-task.
- Tie each category to a different control posture: simple IDE use needs one set of guardrails; terminal agents and cloud agents need stronger approvals, sandboxing, logging, and branch protection.
- Treat feature, preview, benchmark, and autonomy claims as dated evidence rather than stable product truths.
